exports.content: Deep-level traps at the buried perovskite/HTL interface in mixed Pb-Sn NBG subcells cause severe non-radiative recombination. Conventional long-chain amine passivation improves Voc but impairs charge transport, reducing Jsc and FF due to insulating barriers.
exports.label: buried_interface_problem

exports.content: Dipolar-passivated devices show no significant PCE degradation after over 1000 hours in a nitrogen glovebox without encapsulation
exports.label: dark_storage_1000h

exports.content: Dipolar passivation extends carrier diffusion length to 6.2 um (versus 4.8 um for control) with mobility of 113.5 cm2 V-1 s-1 (versus 67.5 cm2 V-1 s-1), as measured by terahertz spectroscopy
exports.label: diffusion_length_62um

exports.content: A dipolar-passivation strategy using sulfanilic acid (SA) at the HTL/Pb-Sn perovskite interface reduces trap density while enabling precise energy-level alignment through oriented dipoles, simultaneously suppressing recombination and enhancing carrier extraction
exports.label: dipolar_passivation_strategy

exports.content: Dipolar-passivation devices show electroluminescence quantum yield of 7.05% versus 2.40% for controls at 1-sun current density, corresponding to Voc loss reduction from 103 mV to 73 mV
exports.label: elqy_705

exports.content: All-perovskite tandem solar cells with WBG (~1.78 eV) FA0.8Cs0.2Pb(I0.62Br0.38)3 top cell and NBG (~1.25 eV) Pb-Sn perovskite bottom cell. Device areas 0.049 cm2 and 1.05 cm2. Certified by JET (Japan Electrical Safety and Environment Technology Laboratories). PEDOT:PSS HTL with sulfanilic acid (SA) dipolar passivation.
exports.label: exp_context

exports.content: Large-area all-perovskite tandem (1.05 cm2) achieves certified stabilized PCE of 29.6%, demonstrating scalability
exports.label: large_tandem_296

exports.content: Dipolar-passivated Pb-Sn PSCs achieve 24.9% PCE with Voc of 0.911 V, Jsc of 33.1 mA cm-2, and FF of 82.6%, representing one of the highest reported PCEs for mixed Pb-Sn PSCs
exports.label: nbg_pce_249

exports.content: The oriented SA dipoles create type-II energy-level alignment with ohmic contact at the HTL/Pb-Sn perovskite interface, facilitating efficient hole injection into PEDOT:PSS while repelling electrons from the interface
exports.label: ohmic_contact

exports.content: Dipolar passivation improves QFLS from 904 meV to 940 meV at the HTL/perovskite interface (full device stack), with the primary QFLS loss reduction attributed to enhanced HTL interface quality
exports.label: qfls_improvement

exports.content: SA molecules adopt a preferred orientation with -NH3+ anchoring to the perovskite bottom surface and -SO3- directed towards PEDOT:PSS, confirmed by AIMD simulations and KPFM measurements showing surface potential decrease from -80 mV to -162 mV
exports.label: sa_molecular_orientation

exports.content: All-perovskite tandem solar cells with dipolar passivation achieve 30.6% PCE (certified stabilized 30.1% by JET) for 0.049 cm2 area
exports.label: tandem_pce_306

exports.content: Dipolar-passivation tandem devices show average Voc 50 mV higher than controls, with average PCE of 30.3 +/- 0.3% versus 29.1 +/- 0.2%
exports.label: tandem_voc_50mv_gain

exports.content: The wide-bandgap subcell achieves 20.5% PCE with Voc of 1.329 V, Jsc of 18.4 mA cm-2, and FF of 83.8%, using SAM-modified NiO HTL
exports.label: wbg_subcell_205
